meta_title: Impact Windows in Kings Point, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact windows in Kings Point, FL. Protect your condo from 170mph winds, lower insurance costs & save on energy bills.
h1: Impact Windows Built for Kings Point Living — Protection, Comfort, and Peace of Mind
intro:
Kings Point is one of Palm Beach County's most beloved 55+ communities, a meticulously maintained neighborhood in Boynton Beach where residents have built their retirement lives around comfort, security, and community. Nestled around the iconic Kings Point Clubhouse and Theater, this vibrant condominium community offers a lifestyle that deserves the same level of care when it comes to home protection. That care starts from the outside in — and it starts with impact windows designed specifically for South Florida's demanding climate.

Although Kings Point sits approximately six miles from the Atlantic coastline, that distance offers far less protection than many homeowners assume. Palm Beach County sits in a Wind Zone rated for 170 mph sustained winds, meaning every structure here — from ground-floor condominiums to upper-level units — must be properly fortified against the kind of catastrophic pressure changes and windborne debris that South Florida storms regularly produce. With roughly 70% of homes in the Kings Point area built before 1994, many units were constructed before today's modern hurricane protection standards were established, leaving thousands of residents exposed to risk that current Florida Building Code requirements were specifically designed to address.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has helped hundreds of homeowners and condo owners across Palm Beach County upgrade from outdated single-pane or slider windows to fully certified, hurricane-rated impact windows. For Kings Point residents specifically, the benefits go well beyond storm protection. Properly installed impact windows dramatically reduce outside noise — a meaningful quality-of-life improvement in a densely populated condominium setting. They also cut cooling costs significantly during South Florida's nine-month summer season and can generate substantial annual savings on homeowners or condo insurance premiums. With financing options starting as low as $87 per month with $0 down, there has never been a more accessible time to make this critical upgrade.

section_why.h2: Why Kings Point Residents Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing as Their Local Window Specialists
section_why.body:
Choosing a contractor for impact window installation in a condominium community like Kings Point requires more than just picking a company with a flashy ad. It demands a team that understands the unique structural and regulatory nuances of multi-unit residential buildings, one that knows how to coordinate with HOA requirements, condominium association boards, and the Palm Beach County Building Division permit process — all without disrupting the daily routines of the residents around you. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings all of that expertise to every Kings Point project.

Unlike many companies that rely on subcontractors, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing uses 100% in-house installation crews. Our team carries over 60 years of combined experience installing impact windows and doors throughout Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. That means the same licensed, insured professionals who quote your job are the ones who show up on installation day. There are no handoffs to third-party laborers unfamiliar with your building, no gaps in accountability, and no surprises when it comes to quality. Our A+ BBB rating and more than 500 completed homes across South Florida reflect the standard we hold ourselves to on every single project.

For Kings Point condo owners, we also understand that window replacement often involves working within specific architectural guidelines or unit-by-unit approvals. Our team is experienced in navigating these processes smoothly, providing all necessary documentation — including product approval numbers, Notice of Commencement filings, and permit applications — so you never have to chase paperwork. We handle every detail from the initial measurement to the final inspection sign-off with the Palm Beach County Building Division.

We are proud to be a true South Florida company serving a South Florida community. We know Kings Point, we know its neighboring cities of Delray Beach, Boynton Beach, and Boca Raton, and we understand exactly what it means to protect a home in this region. When you work with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you are working with the Kings of Service — a team that treats your home with the same respect and commitment we would give our own.

section_history.h2: A Storm History That Demands Serious Hurricane Protection in Kings Point
section_history.body:
Kings Point residents do not have to look far into the past to understand the very real threat that tropical weather poses to their community. Since 1990, the greater Boynton Beach and Palm Beach County area has been impacted by at least seven named storms, each one a reminder that South Florida's tropical geography makes hurricane preparedness a year-round responsibility rather than a seasonal afterthought.

Three storms stand out in the recent memory of Kings Point residents. Hurricane Frances made landfall in September 2004, battering Palm Beach County with sustained winds and prolonged heavy rainfall that left widespread roof and window damage across the region. Just one year later, Hurricane Wilma struck in October 2005 as one of the most intense Atlantic hurricanes ever recorded, producing catastrophic wind damage throughout South Florida. Wilma's rapid intensification and unexpected speed caught many property owners off guard, and the resulting damage claims in Palm Beach County alone reached into the billions. Then came Hurricane Irma in September 2017, a storm that tracked directly through the Florida peninsula and reminded an entire new generation of homeowners — many of whom had moved to communities like Kings Point during calmer years — just how serious tropical storm exposure remains.

For a community where the majority of residential units were built before current hurricane standards were implemented, these storms represent more than historical footnotes — they are proof that the windows and doors installed decades ago were simply not designed to withstand what South Florida regularly demands. Impact windows from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing are engineered and tested to meet or exceed today's 170 mph wind zone requirements, providing Kings Point residents with a level of protection that older single-pane or accordion shutter systems cannot reliably replicate.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Compliance and the Palm Beach County Permit Process for Impact Windows
section_code.body:
When replacing windows in Kings Point, compliance with the Florida Building Code is not optional — it is a legal requirement that also directly affects your home's insurability and resale value. Kings Point falls under Palm Beach County's jurisdiction, which means all window replacement projects must be permitted through the Palm Beach County Building Division and must meet the wind load and impact resistance standards established under the current Florida Building Code.

While Kings Point is not located within Florida's High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — that designation applies specifically to Miami-Dade and Broward counties — Palm Beach County still mandates hurricane-rated products for all new window installations. Windows must carry a valid Florida Product Approval (FPA) number demonstrating they have been independently tested for large and small missile impact resistance, cyclic wind pressure, and water infiltration. For the 170 mph design wind speed applicable to this area, every window and door unit must be sized, glazed, and anchored to meet calculated structural loads based on the specific opening dimensions and building height.

The permitting process through the Palm Beach County Building Division requires a licensed contractor to submit product approval documentation, installation specifications, and in many cases energy compliance calculations under the Florida Energy Code. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages this entire process on behalf of every Kings Point client. We pull the permits, submit the required product documentation, schedule the required inspections, and ensure your installation closes out with a completed inspection record — giving you a clean title and full code compliance that matters when it comes time to sell your unit, refinance, or file an insurance claim.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Windows: The Products We Install in Kings Point Homes
section_products.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we install two industry-trusted brands for our Kings Point customers: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both product lines are rigorously tested and Florida Product Approved, engineered to meet the demanding wind load requirements of Palm Beach County's 170 mph wind zone while delivering the comfort and efficiency features that today's homeowners expect from a premium window upgrade.

ESW impact windows feature multi-chamber extruded aluminum or vinyl frames that provide exceptional structural rigidity without unnecessary bulk. The laminated glass systems used across the ESW product line incorporate a shatter-resistant interlayer that holds glass fragments in place even under direct impact — meaning that in the event of windborne debris contact, the window maintains its integrity and continues to protect the interior of your home from pressure differentials that can destroy roofs and interior walls. Many ESW configurations also incorporate Low-E glass coatings that reflect infrared heat while allowing natural light to pass through, reducing solar heat gain and cutting air conditioning costs that are a significant monthly expense in Kings Point's year-round warm climate.

CWI impact windows offer comparable performance with an emphasis on slim sightlines and architectural versatility — particularly important in a condominium setting where window profiles may need to match existing unit aesthetics or HOA-approved specifications. CWI products carry Florida Product Approval certifications and are available in a wide range of configurations including single-hung, casement, sliding, and picture window styles. Together, ESW and CWI give our Kings Point customers access to the right product for every opening in their home, installed by a team that knows these systems inside and out.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Windows Cost in Kings Point — And How Financing Makes It Accessible
section_cost.body:
One of the most common questions we receive from Kings Point residents is straightforward: what is this going to cost me? The honest answer depends on the number of windows being replaced, the sizes and configurations of each opening, and any structural or HOA-related considerations specific to your unit. For a typical Kings Point condominium — which may range from a one-bedroom unit with four to six windows to a larger two-bedroom corner unit with ten or more openings — full impact window replacement projects commonly range from approximately $8,000 to $20,000 or more, depending on scope.

In the context of Kings Point's average home value of approximately $220,000, that investment typically represents between 4% and 9% of the property's value — and the return on that investment tends to be significant. Impact windows consistently increase appraised values, improve marketability in a competitive 55+ community real estate market, and generate ongoing savings through lower utility bills and reduced insurance premiums. Many Kings Point homeowners report annual insurance savings of $500 to over $1,500 per year following a verified wind mitigation inspection, meaning the investment begins paying for itself from the very first renewal cycle.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes this upgrade accessible to middle-income homeowners through flexible financing options that require $0 down and start as low as $87 per month. We work with multiple lending partners to match Kings Point residents with terms that fit their budget, whether that means a short-term payoff plan or a longer-term option that keeps monthly payments manageable. When you factor in energy savings, insurance discounts, and increased home equity, impact windows are not just a safety upgrade — they are a financially sound decision that protects your investment on multiple fronts.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings and Wind Mitigation Credits for Kings Point Impact Window Owners
section_insurance.body:
Homeowners and condo unit owners in Kings Point have a powerful financial incentive to invest in impact windows beyond storm protection alone — and that incentive comes directly from the insurance industry. Florida law requires insurance carriers to offer wind mitigation credits to policyholders who have verified hurricane protection features installed on their homes, and impact windows are one of the highest-value qualifying features on the wind mitigation inspection form used across Palm Beach County.

Once your new impact windows are installed and your permit is closed out with the Palm Beach County Building Division, you can request a wind mitigation inspection from a licensed inspector. The resulting report documents your window ratings, frame construction, and roof-to-wall connections, and is submitted directly to your insurance carrier. Most major carriers writing homeowners or HO-6 condo policies in South Florida — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Insurance, and others — apply meaningful premium discounts based on the opening protection credits identified in that report. Depending on your current policy structure and carrier, Kings Point residents frequently see annual savings ranging from several hundred dollars to well over $1,000 per year.

For condo unit owners, it is important to understand the distinction between what your association's master policy covers and what your individual HO-6 unit owner policy covers. In many cases, the interior of your windows — the glass, frames, and hardware as part of your unit — falls under your personal policy rather than the master building policy. This means the wind mitigation credits from your new impact windows can directly reduce your own annual premium. Our team is happy to walk you through what documentation you will need and how to communicate your upgrade to your insurance agent to ensure you capture every discount you have earned.

faqs.q: Do I need HOA or condo association approval to replace windows in Kings Point?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. Kings Point is a condominium community governed by association rules that typically require unit owners to submit product specifications and receive written approval before any window replacement work begins. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is experienced in preparing the documentation your association needs — including product approval numbers, color and finish specifications, and contractor licensing information — to streamline the approval process and avoid delays on your project.

faqs.q: How much does it cost to replace impact windows in a Kings Point condo, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Costs vary based on the number and size of openings, but most Kings Point condo replacement projects fall in the range of $8,000 to $20,000. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down through multiple lending partners. When you factor in annual insurance savings and lower utility bills, many homeowners find the net monthly cost of ownership is substantially lower than the sticker price suggests.

faqs.q: Is a permit required to replace windows in Kings Point, and who handles that?
faqs.a: Yes, a building permit issued by the Palm Beach County Building Division is required for all window replacement work in Kings Point. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf — from submitting product approval documentation and installation specifications to scheduling required inspections and closing out the permit upon completion. You will receive full documentation of the closed permit for your records.

faqs.q: How long does impact window installation typically take for a Kings Point condo unit?
faqs.a: For a standard condo unit with six to ten windows, installation is typically completed in one to two days by our in-house crew. The overall project timeline from signed contract to completed installation — including the permitting and any HOA approval steps — generally runs four to eight weeks depending on permit processing times and association review schedules. We provide a clear project timeline at the start of every job so you know exactly what to expect.

faqs.q: Will new impact windows really lower my homeowners insurance premium in Kings Point?
faqs.a: Yes, in most cases they will. After installation, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can document your new impact-rated windows on the standard OIR-B1-1802 wind mitigation form. This report is submitted to your insurance carrier, who is required by Florida law to apply applicable opening protection credits to your premium calculation. Kings Point condo owners with HO-6 policies frequently see annual premium reductions ranging from a few hundred dollars to over $1,000 depending on their carrier and current coverage structure.

cta_body: Call Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free, no-obligation estimate — our in-house experts will assess your Kings Point condo, walk you through your product options, and provide transparent pricing with flexible financing starting at $0 down. Experience why we are The Kings of Service.
